ABOUT

I've sat inside the chaos I now automate.

For the last few years I've been the operations layer behind founders and executives — filtering inboxes, running calendars, chasing follow-ups, and keeping CRMs honest. That seat taught me exactly where a business quietly leaks time: the lead that sits three days without a reply, the follow-up someone meant to send, the recording that never gets turned into content.

Now I build the systems that close those gaps — in Zapier, Make, and Asana, with AI doing the parts that used to take a person an afternoon. I'm not a developer who picked up business skills. I'm an operator who learned to build.

FROM THE FIELD

Calendar chaos → control

A CEO was running 9–12 meetings a day, with follow-ups falling through constantly. I audited every meeting type, introduced async updates and booking rules, and built a follow-up tracker.

−40%meetings on the calendar
Fasterdecision cycles
Zeromissed commitments
SELECTED BUILDS

Four systems, four different bottlenecks.

PROJECT 01
Zapier + MakeAI ContentMulti-platform Publishing

One recording becomes a week of content

Challenge

A recorded video sat in a Drive folder while blog posts, captions, and social images waited to be made by hand.

Build

A Drive trigger catches new .mp4 files. AI transcribes the video and drafts blog posts, then branches into platform paths — AI-generated images per post, with Facebook, Instagram, and LinkedIn publishing once content clears review. Built twice: once in Zapier, once in Make.

Result

One upload now produces a transcript, blog posts, social images, and published posts across three platforms — with zero copy-pasting.

PROJECT 02
MakeAI AgentFacebook Pages

Customer messages get answered before the team even sees them

Challenge

Facebook Page inquiries piled up outside business hours, and most replies were the same answer typed out again.

Build

A webhook catches incoming messages and hands them to an AI agent (Gemini) trained on the company's own knowledge base, with memory to keep the conversation coherent, then sends the reply back through the page automatically.

Result

Common questions get an instant, on-brand answer around the clock — the team only steps in for what actually needs a person.

PROJECT 03
ZapierAsanaCRM

Every lead stage has a job to do, automatically

Challenge

Leads moved through five stages in Asana, but follow-ups depended on someone remembering to send them — and someone always forgot.

Build

Updating a lead's status now triggers its own path: a lead folder and content task on kickoff, a two-step nurture sequence on no response, quote follow-ups, a welcome email and PDF on approval, and a referral ask once paid and closed.

Result

Every stage now follows up on itself — nothing waits on someone's memory.

PROJECT 04
ZapierApolloLead Routing

Leads arrive already qualified

Challenge

New leads landed with just a name and an email — sales had to research each one before knowing if it was worth their time.

Build

A webhook catches the new lead, pulls the company URL, enriches it through Apollo, then splits by priority — high-priority leads get saved, Slacked to sales, and an AI-drafted outreach email; lower-priority leads get a quieter notification.

Result

Sales opens a Slack message already knowing who the lead is — with an email already drafted.
